While Southern Ontario or Quebec have similar deciduous trees, the overall landscape and vegetation density are less characteristic of the Canadian prairie or boreal transition zones.

The utility poles in the USA are predominantly wooden in rural areas; concrete poles of this specific design are not standard for rural US roads.
